edges.description: Abnormal persistence of fetal pro-patency signaling impairs normal postnatal ductal closure.
edges.source: Prostaglandin and oxygen-tension ductal closure signaling

edges.description: A persistently patent ductus arteriosus permits ongoing aortopulmonary shunting after birth.
Reduced ductus arteriosus smooth muscle tone and contractility impairs functional closure.
edges.source: Ductus arteriosus smooth muscle contractility program

edges.target: Prostaglandin and oxygen-tension ductal closure signaling
Prostaglandin and oxygen-tension ductal closure signaling

nodes.description: Acetaminophen is used as an alternative pharmacologic closure or prophylaxis strategy in preterm infants, with clinical trials evaluating prevention of ductal patency and severe morbidity.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of the route of administration on the efficacy and safety of the drug therapy for patent ductus arteriosus in premature infants: a systematic review and meta-analysis
evidence.snippet: the use of paracetamol demonstrated no significant difference in PDA closure and the risk of adverse events between oral and intravenous administration.

nodes.description: Developmental evidence indicates that ductus arteriosus smooth muscle identity and contractility are necessary for ductal closure; impaired contractility can produce a patent ductus arteriosus phenotype.
nodes.id: Ductus arteriosus smooth muscle contractility program

evidence.reference_title: Prdm6 drives ductus arteriosus closure by promoting ductus arteriosus smooth muscle cell identity and contractility.
evidence.snippet: Prdm6 depletion in Wnt1-expressing cells during development resulted in perinatal lethality and a completely penetrant patent ductus arteriosus (DA) phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Prdm6 drives ductus arteriosus closure by promoting ductus arteriosus smooth muscle cell identity and contractility.
evidence.snippet: Prdm6 depletion significantly reduced DA tone and contractility.

nodes.description: When persistent ductal flow is large enough, pulmonary overcirculation and systemic hypoperfusion can define hemodynamically significant PDA and increase risk for adverse neonatal outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Patent ductus arteriosus (also non-hemodynamically significant) correlates with poor outcomes in very low birth weight infants. A multicenter cohort study
evidence.snippet: A diagnosis of hemodynamically significant PDA (hsPDA) was established when the PDA diameter was ≥ 1.6 mm at the pulmonary end with growing or pulsatile flow pattern, and at least 2 of 3 indexes of pulmonary overcirculation and/or systemic hypoperfusion were present.

nodes.description: Cyclooxygenase inhibitor therapy with ibuprofen is a common medical closure strategy in premature infants, with evidence varying by route of administration.

evidence.reference_title: The impact of the route of administration on the efficacy and safety of the drug therapy for patent ductus arteriosus in premature infants: a systematic review and meta-analysis
evidence.snippet: oral ibuprofen may offer certain advantages in closing PDA without increasing the risk of adverse events.

nodes.description: The ductus arteriosus normally functions as a fetal vascular connection and closes after birth through coordinated ductal constriction and remodeling. Failure of this patency-control program leaves an aortopulmonary channel.

evidence.reference_title: Ductus Arteriosus in Fetal and Perinatal Life
evidence.snippet: The ductus arteriosus represents an essential vascular structure connecting the pulmonary artery and the aorta.

evidence.reference_title: Ductus Arteriosus in Fetal and Perinatal Life
evidence.snippet: the regulatory mechanisms governing ductal patency in critical stages such as the fetal and the perinatal period

nodes.description: Fetal ductal patency is maintained by vasoactive signaling, including prostaglandin-mediated relaxation. The postnatal fall in prostaglandin tone and rise in oxygen tension normally shift the ductus arteriosus toward functional constriction; persistent pro-patency signaling contributes to PDA.
nodes.id: Prostaglandin and oxygen-tension ductal closure signaling

evidence.snippet: Normal physiologic mechanisms contributing to closure - oxygen tension and decreased prostaglandins-are altered in prematurity.
The maintenance of ductal patency is essential for the normal development of the fetus.
